projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — commissioning inspections and final HV equipment QAQC ongoing.
projects.summary: Large-scale wind farm under construction, featuring 1026 MW capacity. Includes a new 330kV substation and associated HV transmission lines. Turbine installation is ongoing, with commissioning expected late 2025 / early 2026, creating immediate needs for HV equipment FAT, QAQC surveillance, and commissioning inspection for transformers, circuit breakers, and switchgear.
projects.statusNote: Construction well underway, first turbines installed early 2024. Commissioning expected late 2025 / early 2026. (cleanco.com.au, reneweconomy.com.au, 2024)

projects.projectName: Swan Hill Solar Farm and BESS
projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — BESS and solar park commissioning, HV substation works.
projects.summary: 132 MW solar farm co-located with a 100 MW / 200 MWh BESS. FID announced and construction commenced in late 2023, targeting completion late 2024 / early 2025. This involves ongoing procurement and installation of HV equipment for grid connection, including transformers and switchgear for the substation.
projects.statusNote: Risen Energy announced FID and construction commencement in October 2023, targeting completion early 2025. (pv-magazine-australia.com, 2023)

projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — substation and HV equipment FAT and QAQC.
projects.summary: 250 MW solar PV project combined with a 250 MWh BESS. FID announced in March 2024, with construction starting H2 2024 and commissioning targeted for late 2025. Project includes a new 220kV substation and transmission line connection to Gannawarra Terminal Station, requiring HV transformers, circuit breakers, and switchgear.
projects.statusNote: FRV Australia announced FID in March 2024, with construction commencing H2 2024. (frvaustralia.com.au, 2024)

projects.location: Wagga Wagga, NSW to Robertstown, SA
projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — ongoing HV substation equipment FAT, QAQC, and site supervision.
projects.summary: Critical 330kV interconnector project between NSW and SA, facilitating up to 800MW transfer. Construction is advanced, with major sections of transmission line and new substations (e.g., Buronga, Dinawan) progressing rapidly in 2024. Commissioning is staged through late 2025 / 2026, necessitating ongoing FAT and inspection of HV transformers, circuit breakers, and switchgear.
projects.statusNote: Construction is well underway across multiple sections, significant progress in 2024. Commissioning expected late 2025 / 2026. (transgrid.com.au, 2024)

projects.projectName: New England Renewable Energy Zone (REZ) Transmission Infrastructure
projects.mpEngagementWindow: Engage H2 2025 — position for major HV equipment FAT post-EPC award.
projects.summary: Development of backbone transmission infrastructure for the 8 GW New England REZ. Early works are ongoing, with major EPC for the network infrastructure (new 330kV lines and substations) expected to be awarded in 2025. This presents significant future opportunities for HV equipment FAT, QAQC, and site supervision for transformers, circuit breakers, and GIS.
projects.statusNote: EnergyCo NSW is progressing with transmission backbone, major EPC expected 2025. (energyco.nsw.gov.au, 2024)

projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — ongoing HV substation equipment FAT, QAQC, and site supervision.
projects.summary: Major 500kV overhead transmission line and substation project. Construction is well underway, with multiple packages awarded and works continuing through 2025 and 2026. Significant HV substation equipment (transformers, 500kV circuit breakers, GIS) procurement and installation are ongoing, requiring FAT, QAQC, and site supervision.
projects.statusNote: Construction progressing across multiple sections in 2024, targeting 2026 completion. (transgrid.com.au, 2024)

projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — continuous demand for HV equipment FAT and inspection for new phases/sites.
projects.summary: Continuous expansion of Microsoft's hyperscale data centre footprint in Western Sydney. New facilities and phases are under construction or in advanced planning, requiring substantial new high-voltage grid connections and dedicated substations with HV transformers and switchgear.
projects.statusNote: Microsoft is continuously expanding its Azure region with new facilities in Western Sydney, demanding new high-voltage grid infrastructure. (afr.com, 2024)

projects.projectName: Vena Energy - Wandoan South BESS Expansion
projects.mpEngagementWindow: Engage H2 2025 — early positioning for BESS and HV equipment FAT for future stages.
projects.summary: Planned significant expansion of the Wandoan South BESS, potentially up to 450 MW / 1800 MWh. Stage 1 is commissioned, but later stages are in advanced planning, which will require new BESS procurement and associated HV grid connection upgrades, including transformers and switchgear.
projects.statusNote: Stage 1 is online. Vena Energy is actively progressing plans for significant expansion up to 450 MW / 1800 MWh. (venaenergy.com, 2024)

projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — ongoing HV substation equipment FAT, QAQC, and site supervision for 330kV assets.
projects.summary: Development of key transmission infrastructure for the 3 GW Central-West Orana REZ. Construction commenced late 2023 / early 2024, including new 330kV transmission lines and substations (Wellington, Merotherie). Significant HV equipment procurement, installation, and commissioning will continue for several years.
projects.statusNote: Construction commenced late 2023 / early 2024 with consortium awarded network infrastructure. (energyco.nsw.gov.au, 2024)

projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — commissioning inspection and HV equipment FAT/QAQC.
projects.summary: Hyperscale data centre (BNE3) under construction with 72 MW IT load and 130 MVA total capacity. Targeting completion in early 2025. This facility includes significant HV power infrastructure, requiring new grid connections, substation equipment like transformers, and switchgear, leading to ongoing FAT and commissioning opportunities.
projects.statusNote: NEXTDC's BNE3 facility is under construction, slated for completion in H1 2025. (nextdc.com, 2024)

projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — BESS module QAQC, HV equipment FAT and site supervision.
projects.summary: 100 MW / 200 MWh BESS project. FID announced in February 2024, with construction to commence mid-2024 and targeted operation in late 2025. This involves procurement of BESS modules and significant HV substation connection works to the 220kV transmission network, requiring FAT for transformers and circuit breakers.
projects.statusNote: Synergy announced FID for Port Hedland BESS in February 2024, construction mid-2024, operation late 2025. (synergy.net.au, 2024)

projects.epcContractor: McConnell Dowell and John Holland JV (K2-Hydro)
projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — ongoing HV transmission and substation equipment FAT and QAQC, commissioning support.
projects.summary: 250 MW pumped hydro storage (K2-Hydro) and 50 MW solar PV (KSPP). K2-Hydro and the associated 186km 275kV transmission line are under construction, targeting completion in 2025. This project involves substantial HV equipment for the hydro plant, new substations, and the transmission line, requiring ongoing FAT, QAQC, and commissioning support for HV transformers, circuit breakers, and switchgear.
projects.statusNote: K2-Hydro and 275kV transmission line under construction, targeting completion 2025. (genexpower.com.au, 2024)

projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — ongoing HV equipment FAT for Stage 1 and site supervision, prepare for Stage 2 procurement.
projects.summary: Large-scale BESS project, 500 MW / 2000 MWh planned capacity. Stage 1 (200 MW / 800 MWh) is under construction, expected online late 2024. Stage 2 (300 MW / 1200 MWh) is in advanced approvals and procurement. This includes significant HV connection infrastructure, demanding ongoing FAT and QAQC for HV transformers, circuit breakers, and switchgear.
projects.statusNote: Stage 1 under construction, expected online late 2024. Stage 2 in advanced procurement. (synergy.net.au, pv-magazine-australia.com, 2024)

projects.location: Hunter Valley, NSW to QLD border
projects.mpEngagementWindow: Engage H1 2026 — position for major HV equipment FAT and QAQC.
projects.summary: Proposed 500kV interconnector and transmission line, forming a crucial link for the Hunter-Central Coast REZ. Currently in advanced planning and studies, with major procurement for HV transmission lines and substations (including HV transformers, circuit breakers, and GIS) expected to commence in 2026.
projects.statusNote: TransGrid progressing studies and approvals, major procurement for HV transmission and substations expected H2 2025 / 2026. (transgrid.com.au, 2024)

projects.projectName: Eraring Green Energy Hub (BESS Stage 2 & Hydrogen)
projects.location: Eraring Power Station, New South Wales
projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — tender active for Stage 2 BESS modules and HV connection components.
projects.summary: Expansion of the Eraring Green Energy Hub with a planned 700 MW / 1400 MWh BESS (Stage 2) and an early-stage hydrogen electrolyser pilot. While Stage 1 is under construction, Stage 2 is in procurement for BESS modules and related HV connection infrastructure. This involves FAT for HV equipment and QAQC surveillance for battery components.
projects.statusNote: Origin committed to Stage 2 BESS, seeking partners for BESS module supply and installation. (reneweconomy.com.au, 2024)

projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — commissioning inspection and HV equipment QAQC.
projects.summary: 504 MW wind complex under construction, targeting operation in 2024/2025. Turbine installation is ongoing, with commissioning and grid connection imminent. The project includes a new substation and associated HV transmission infrastructure, requiring FAT and inspection of HV transformers, circuit breakers, and switchgear.
projects.statusNote: Construction is advanced, with turbine installation ongoing and commissioning expected 2024/2025. (casadosventos.com.br, CanalEnergia, 2024)

projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — commissioning inspection and HV equipment QAQC.
projects.summary: 475 MWp solar PV project under construction, targeting operation in Q1 2025. The project involves connection to a 500kV transmission line, necessitating a new substation and substantial HV equipment, including transformers and switchgear, with ongoing FAT and commissioning inspection needs.
projects.statusNote: Construction well underway, commissioning expected early 2025. (eneva.com.br, CanalEnergia, 2024)

projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — wind farm commissioning and HV equipment QAQC, prepare for solar phase procurement.
projects.summary: Hybrid wind (600 MW) and solar (250 MW planned) complex. FID for the wind phase was reached in Q4 2023, with construction underway in 2024 and expected operation in 2025. The project includes significant new HV transmission infrastructure and substations, requiring ongoing FAT and QAQC for HV transformers, circuit breakers, and switchgear.
projects.statusNote: Atlas announced FID for wind phase in Q4 2023, construction underway 2024, expected operation 2025. (atlasrenewableenergy.com, 2024)

projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — continuous demand for HV equipment FAT, QAQC for new solar phases.
projects.summary: Ongoing expansion of one of Latin America's largest solar complexes, with new phases continually under construction or in advanced planning for completion 2025-2026. This requires additional HV equipment procurement, including transformers and switchgear for new substations and grid connections.
projects.statusNote: Ongoing expansion with new phases under construction for 2025-2026 completion. (elerarenovaveis.com.br, CanalEnergia, 2024)

projects.location: Multiple locations (e.g., São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro)
projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — continuous demand for HV equipment FAT/QAQC for new data centres.
projects.summary: Continuous expansion of hyperscale data centre portfolio across Brazil. Several new facilities or significant expansions (e.g., Sumaré 3, Vinhedo 3) are under construction or in advanced planning for 2025-2026. These projects consistently require dedicated substations, new high-voltage grid connections, and HV equipment (transformers, switchgear, circuit breakers).
projects.statusNote: Ascenty investing BRL 2.5 billion in new facilities and expansions for 2024/2025, requiring robust power infrastructure. (ascenty.com, Valor Econômico, 2024)

projects.projectName: Complexo Eólico Lagoa do Barro Expansion
projects.location: Lagoa do Barro do Piauí, Piauí
projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — commissioning inspection and HV equipment QAQC for expansion.
projects.summary: Expansion of the Lagoa do Barro Wind Complex (Phase 2), with targeted operation by 2025. Following Atlas Renewable Energy's acquisition, development and construction of expansion phases are underway, involving new substations and grid connections. This creates demand for HV equipment FAT and QAQC for transformers and switchgear.
projects.statusNote: Expansion (Phase 2) under construction, targeting operation 2025. Atlas acquired portfolio in 2024. (atlasrenewableenergy.com, CanalEnergia, 2024)

projects.mpEngagementWindow: Engage H2 2025 — EPC tender and HV equipment procurement expected.
projects.summary: Planned 250 MWp solar PV project, with FID expected in H2 2024 and construction planned to start H1 2025. This project includes a new substation and grid connection, implying future procurement needs for HV transformers, circuit breakers, and switchgear.
projects.statusNote: Rio Energy announced advanced plans for Candiota solar, FID expected late 2024, construction 2025. (CanalEnergia, 2024)

projects.location: Pecém Industrial and Port Complex, Ceará
projects.mpEngagementWindow: Engage H1 2026 — position for electrolyser and associated HV equipment procurement.
projects.summary: Development of a large-scale green hydrogen hub with multi-GW potential for electrolysers, supported by new renewable energy generation (wind/solar) and extensive HV grid infrastructure. Multiple projects are in various stages of planning and early development, with large-scale FIDs expected from 2025 onwards, requiring significant HV transmission and substation equipment.
projects.statusNote: Pecém is a hub for green hydrogen, multiple projects in planning/permitting, large-scale FIDs expected 2025+. (ceara.gov.br, CanalEnergia, 2024)

projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — ongoing HV equipment FAT and QAQC, site supervision.
projects.summary: 345km, 500kV transmission line connecting Piauí and Ceará, awarded in ANEEL auctions. Currently under construction, aiming for completion in 2025. This project includes new substations and substantial HV equipment (transformers, circuit breakers, switchgear), requiring ongoing FAT and QAQC, and site supervision.
projects.statusNote: 500kV transmission line under construction, targeting completion 2025. (ANEEL, CanalEnergia, 2024)

projects.projectName: Rio do Vento Wind and Solar Complex (Phase 2)
projects.mpEngagementWindow: Immediate — ongoing HV equipment FAT, QAQC for wind/solar components.
projects.summary: Multi-phase wind and solar complex with over 1 GW total capacity. Phase 2 (wind and solar) is currently under construction, with completion expected in 2025. This phase involves new substations and grid connections, requiring ongoing FAT and QAQC for HV transformers, circuit breakers, and switchgear for both wind and solar components.
projects.statusNote: Phase 2 (wind and solar) underway, completion expected 2025. (casadosventos.com.br, CanalEnergia, 2024)
